reason: SRS faithfully transcribes SPEC.md v2.0.0 in declared INGESTION mode: all 3 FRs (FR-01..FR-03) and 3 NFRs (NFR-01..NFR-03) present and accurate, validation/injection-blacklist/redaction-regex/exit-codes/entry-point/config-authority all match canonical. Test inventory is comprehensive (FR-01 validation, FR-02 state machine, FR-03 subcommands, security red team, KPI gates, reliability). Stakeholders (primary user, methodology reviewers, owner) and constraints (3-FR/3-NFR immutable, stdlib only, out-of-scope) all surfaced. Two minor cosmetic inconsistencies noted; neither affects traceability or testability.
citations: SRS.md:130-135 (configuration: 3 TASKQ_* env vars with defaults)
SRS.md:118-123 (constraints: immutable FRs/NFRs, stdlib only, SoT, out-of-scope)

gaps.message: SRS self-declares 'INGESTION (100% transcription, no invention)' yet adds 'Implementation Function (est.)' column and 'implementation_functions' JSON entries that are not in SPEC.md (e.g., taskq.store.submit_task, taskq.executor.run, taskq.cli.main). These are reasonable estimates and do not contradict the spec, but slightly violate the declared authoring mode; consider adding '(estimated; not in SPEC.md)' disclaimer.

Naming inconsistency in FR-02 implementation function: the table at line ~45 lists 'taskq.executor.run' but the FR-02 JSON entry at line ~206 lists 'taskq.executor.run_task'. Pick one canonical name (taskq.executor.run_task reads more Pythonic and matches the run/store/clear verb pattern, but consistency is the priority).
